title: Question: Problems compiling generated C++
body:
---


Author Name: **Sven Stucki**
Original Redmine Message: 1662 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi

I'm just starting with Verilator and I have some issues with the generated C++ code.

Running the example file from the manual works fine. But if I try to use Verilator for my existing code I get the following error when compiling:

```
$ make -f Vtb.mk Vtb
...
Vtb.cpp: In static member function 'static void Vtb::_initial__TOP__5(Vtb__Syms*)':
Vtb.cpp:2954:21: error: 'VL_FOPEN_NI' was not declared in this scope
               , 0x77U);

```

I guess it has something to do with @$fopen@ (which is used in the code), but from the manual I figured that it would be supported?

My files all have a .sv extension, do I still need to add @+1800-2012ext+sv@ when running verilator?
code_blocks:
$ make -f Vtb.mk Vtb
...
Vtb.cpp: In static member function 'static void Vtb::_initial__TOP__5(Vtb__Syms*)':
Vtb.cpp:2954:21: error: 'VL_FOPEN_NI' was not declared in this scope
               , 0x77U);
